seo.title: Oregon Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Oregon — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license oregon, dental assistant certification oregon, RDA oregon, DANB CDA oregon, dental assistant radiography oregon

board.name: Oregon Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: Oregon Board of Dentistry, 1500 SW 1st Ave, Suite 770, Portland OR 97201

license_types.title: Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Oregon.

steps.title: Create a Licensing Gateway Account
steps.description: Register for a new account on the Oregon Board of Dentistry's online Licensing Gateway portal to begin your application.

steps.title: Submit Initial Application
steps.description: Log in to the Licensing Gateway and select 'Initial Application' for the specific Dental Assistant certificate you are seeking (e.g., Dental Assistant Certificate, Radiology Health and Safety (RHS), Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)).

steps.title: Provide Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, which may include official transcripts from approved programs, proof of out-of-state certification, verification of work experience, and/or official DANB exam results, directly through the Licensing Gateway.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee for your selected Dental Assistant certificate through the Licensing Gateway.

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Oregon Board of Dentistry will review your complete application and all submitted documentation for compliance with state requirements.

steps.title: Receive Certificate
steps.description: Upon successful review and approval by the Board, your Dental Assistant certificate will be issued, and you can typically view and print it from your Licensing Gateway account.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Oregon Board of Dentistry by certification.

faqs.question: Does Oregon require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Oregon requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Oregon?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Oregon.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Oregon?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Oregon?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Oregon Board of Dentistry by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Oregon
hero.dek: Oregon does not license 'basic' dental assistants; however, certifications for specific expanded functions and radiology are required and handled by the Oregon Board of Dentistry's Licensing Gateway.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the Licensing Gateway URL and click on the 'Create Account' link located in the upper right corner of the page. Once logged in, select 'Initial Application' for the desired Dental Assistant certificate.
